passed_law: License required to use a 3D printer to manufacture firearms; digital firearm manufacturing-code restrictions also apply.
active_proposal: AB 2047 active in Senate Appropriations; would require firearm-blocking / blueprint-detection technology for 3D printers sold in California.
notes: Includes both an existing manufacture restriction and an active printer-level blocking proposal.

passed_law: HB 1144 signed May 4, 2026; prohibits 3D printing firearms/components except by licensed actors.
Bans unlicensed 3D printing of guns and distribution of 3D-printing gun instructions.
State law includes no unlicensed 3D printing of guns.
notes: SB 285 ghost-gun possession/transfer bill was not counted as active because LegiScan marked it introduced/dead.

passed_law: Regulates 3D printers and CNC milling machines for firearm manufacture; only qualifying licensed persons may use them to manufacture or assemble firearms.
notes: Restriction is narrower than a total printer ban.

passed_law: No current state 3D-printing firearm ban found.
active_proposal: SB 331/SB 332 passed Senate and is pending House Government Operations; would ban unlicensed 3D printer/CNC firearm manufacturing.

passed_law: No current state 3D-printing firearm ban found.
active_proposal: HF 3407/SF 3661 would limit 3D printing of guns to federally licensed firearms manufacturers and prohibit distribution of 3D-printer firearm design files; House version died in committee, Senate version reached second reading.
notes: Needs follow-up on Senate version status before relying on it as currently viable.

passed_law: Bans unlicensed 3D printing of guns; bans distribution/possession of 3D-printing instructions; bans plastic undetectable guns.
notes: AB 4975 enacted in 2026 strengthened digital-instruction restrictions.

passed_law: 2026 enacted budget bans using 3D printers to make ghost guns/parts and restricts digital instructions; includes a blocking-technology study and possible future mandate.
active_proposal: Active/introduced bills include S3562/A2228 for 3D-printer purchase background checks and other 3D-printed firearm measures.

passed_law: HB 2320 signed Mar. 24, 2026; bans unlicensed 3D printing/CNC firearm manufacture and regulates digital gun blueprints.
active_proposal: HB 2321 pending in House committee; would require blocking technologies in 3D printers.
